category_title: Preschoolers who use unfriendly or bossy language during peer play
minimal_context: Nia is organizing a play activity with her friends during a free play session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Nia, it is time for the free play session. You can go organize a play activity with your friends.

STUDENT: Let me do that.

TUTOR: Keep playing together.

STUDENT: You aren’t playing right. Stop.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: Get out of my way! Your picture is messy and I can do better than you.

options.A: Use a visual check-in chart where specific numbers, colors, and faces related to anxiety are matched with feeling words.
options.B: Provide written feedback using a 3-point scale ranging from 1 (needs improvement) to 3 (great job) on a daily progress report.
options.C: Teach the student short phrases to say to her friends during the free play session.
options.D: Develop a play plan by identifying the play theme, labeling the toys to be used, and asking the children how they could play.
